sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 46-year-old male, presents with neck pain following a car accident 1 week ago.
He describes the pain as a 7 out of 10, not constant, but worsens with neck movement.
The pain is located in the back of his neck and has not improved with ibuprofen.
He reports occasional headaches and pain with flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending of the neck.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibits positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, with pain on flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending of the neck.
Test Results: X-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain with pain, likely secondary to whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; Suspected whiplash; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for pain management.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to avoid straining his neck and to be conservative with neck movement.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any potential nerve impingement.
I will refer the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will return for further evaluation and management after receiving the results of the MRI.
